jobs.basic_qualifications:
- High school or equivalent
- A minimum of 1 year in an Environmental Health & Safety (EHS) related field OR; A Bachelor’s degree in EHS-relevant background OR; 6 months experience performing in a safety-related role within Amazon
- Knowledge of OSHA regulations specifically 29 CFR 1910
- Experience using Microsoft Word and Excel in a professional capacity
- Experience maintaining confidentiality in matter involving security and/or personnel issues in the workplace
jobs.description:
Join Amazon’s mission to become Earth’s safest place to work! At Amazon, we’ve set the ambitious goal to become the benchmark of safety excellence across all industries in which we operate. As a Workplace Health & Safety Specialist (WHSS), you'll be at the forefront of transforming workplace safety culture at one of the world's most innovative companies. Your expertise and dedication will directly protect employees, ensuring they return home safely to their families every day. You'll facilitate new approaches to safety, combining technology with human-centered solutions to create an environment where safety and productivity thrive together. This isn't just about maintaining safety standards – it's about revolutionizing how we think about and implement workplace safety, making a lasting impact on our people and operations. As your building's designated safety champion, you'll have the unique opportunity to build relationships, influence positive change, and help write the next chapter in Amazon's commitment to becoming Earth's Safest Place to Work.

Core Impact Areas:
Safety Program Excellence & Implementation - Drive comprehensive safety programs through data-driven strategies. Lead daily safety audits, incident investigations, and risk assessments while implementing innovative solutions to prevent injuries. Deliver engaging safety training that resonates with associates and promotes a proactive safety culture. Champion continuous improvement initiatives that enhance workplace safety while maintaining operational efficiency.

Associate Advocacy & Cultural Leadership - Serve as a trusted safety advisor and advocate for associate wellbeing, building strong partnerships across all levels of the organization. Foster a culture where safety is everyone's priority through effective communication, coaching, and positive reinforcement. Develop strong relationships with frontline associates and leadership to drive behavioral changes and ensure safety best practices become natural habits. Transform safety data into compelling narratives that influence positive change and drive engagement in safety initiatives.

Additional Job Elements include:
- Lift up to 49 pounds and frequently push, pull, squat, bend, and reach
- Stand/walk for up to 12 hours during shifts
- Work in an environment where the noise level varies
- Work in an environment that is subject to variable temperatures and weather (delivery stations include outside loading departments)
- Continuously climb and descend stairs (applies to sites with stairs)
- Maintain effective care delivery in emergencies and assist emergency response at the site

Must be available to work flexible shifts including days, nights and/or weekends.

For those that do not have an adult First Aid, CPR, AED certification from the American Heart Association or American Red Cross, must obtain within 3 weeks of start date.

jobs.title: EHS Specialist

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 1+ years of electrical or mechanical experience
- 1+ years’ experience with Microsoft Office products
- Ability to work on a flexible schedule including days, nights, and/or weekends.
jobs.description:
Join our dynamic team and become a critical guardian of technological infrastructure! As a Data Center Engineering Technician, you'll play a pivotal role in maintaining the heartbeat of mission-critical technology infrastructure, ensuring seamless operations that power global digital experiences. Your expertise will directly contribute to maintaining an extraordinary 99.999% uptime, keeping the digital world running smoothly and efficiently.

Engineering Technicians are Amazon’s front line when it comes to hands-on electrical and mechanical equipment troubleshooting. They will maintain, operate, and troubleshoot, Data Center Operation’s Mission Critical Facilities, which includes stand-by diesel generators and related fuel systems, as well as three phase electrical systems such as: switch gear, UPSs, PDUs, wet cell batteries and associated systems, CRAC, centrifugal chillers, cooling towers/water chemical system, air handlers and associated systems, pumps, & motors.

AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

NOTE: Amazon does not sponsor for immigration, including for H-1B, TN, and other non-immigrant visas, for this role.

NOTE: Lump sum stipend will be provided to eligible candidates who relocate for this position.

Key job responsibilities
- Daily building rounds to monitor and record information from electrical, mechanical, and fire/life safety equipment to ensure 24/7 reliability and availability
- Monitor Building Management Systems (BMS) and Electrical Power Management System (EPMS)
- Assist in troubleshooting of facility and rack-level events within internal Service Level Agreements (SLA)
- Perform rack installs and rack decommissioning within internal SLA
- Perform PM, troubleshooting, root cause analysis, and repair of electrical and mechanical equipment
- Technical writing to support change management program
- Monitor daily work requests and manage to resolution, and execute projects from conception to completion
- On-site support of contractors, sub-contractors and vendors, ensuring that all work performed is in accordance with established practices, procedures & local legislation
- Work schedule changes depending on specific site needs. Shifts can be up to 12-hours and may rotate on a predefined schedule. Some locations have on-call rotations.
- Role may support more than one location/site.

Physical Requirements:
Willingness and demonstrated ability to work in the following physical conditions with reasonable accommodations:
- Regularly lift and/or move up to 39 pounds independently
- Participate in group lifts for 40+ pounds
- Maintain balance and perform construction tasks while on a ladder
- Regularly walk, use hands and fingers, handle or feel, reach with hands and arms, stoop, kneel, crouch or crawl, climb stairs, twist, bend, work above shoulders, grasp, carry, push and pull objects into position
- Work in an environment requiring provided ear protection

This role supports data centers in various operational stages. The position may be assigned to a facility still under development, which could require travel to established locations for training until your designated site becomes fully operational.

Essential Requirements:

- Ability to travel to or commute between data center locations as needed

- Willingness to temporarily work at alternative sites during training periods or until assigned facility is operational

- Travel frequency will vary based on business needs and operational status of assigned facility

Candidates must be able to travel (over 50 miles) or commute (less than 50 miles) to active data center sites as required by business needs.

A day in the life
This role acts as Amazon’s front line when it comes to hands-on electrical and mechanical equipment preventative maintenance (PM) and troubleshooting, reporting to a site’s Data Center Facility Manager (FM). They will maintain, operate, and troubleshoot mission-critical data center facility equipment including stand-by diesel generators and related fuel systems, 3 phase electrical systems such as switchgear, UPS units, PDUs, and wet cell batteries and associated systems. Mechanical equipment includes CRAHU units, centrifugal chillers, cooling towers, water systems, air handlers and associated systems, pumps, and motors. Additional support equipment is included in the scope of the role which includes fire/life safety equipment, building automation systems, and general facilities equipment.

About the team
Diverse Experiences AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS? Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture
AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion of events fosters stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship, and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.

jobs.title: Engineering Operation Technician

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 2+ years of employee and performance management experience
- Bachelor's degree or equivalent, or 2+ years of Amazon (blue badge/FTE) experience
- Work a flexible schedule/shift/work area, including weekends, nights, and/or holidays
jobs.description:
Our WW Operations network delivers millions of packages and smiles to Amazon customers every day. We are looking for motivated, customer-focused individuals who want to join our team as an Area Manager. In this role, you will lead a team of hourly workforce and will be responsible for engaging your team during their shifts to maintain the highest levels of safety, quality, attendance, and performance. You will also play a key role in maintaining our customer expectations to ensure customer orders are delivered at the right time, to the right location.

Our Operation’s workflow has three major components: First mile - where the product is housed and ready for order; Middle mile - where the order is hauled to your area; and Last mile - when the product is delivered to the customer’s door. Please note that all workflows have slight building variations, but one thing is constant: our vision and dedication to the customer.

Key job responsibilities
* Support, mentor, and motivate your hourly workforce
* Manage safety, quality, productivity, and customer delivery promises
* Collaborate with all support teams including Safety, Engineering, Loss Prevention, Quality Assurance, Human Resources to develop plans to meet business objectives
* Lift up to 49 pounds and frequently push, pull, squat, bend, and reach
* Stand/walk for up to 12 hours during shifts
* Work in an environment where the noise level varies and can be loud
* Work in an environment that is subject to variable temperatures and weather (delivery stations include outside loading departments)
* Continuously climb and descend stairs (applies to sites with stairs)

Our fulfillment network launches new Operations sites every year, providing various opportunities for your professional growth. We hire Area Managers based on location preference and the business’ current openings.
jobs.description_short: Our WW Operations network delivers millions of packages and smiles to Amazon customers every day. We are looking for motivated, customer-focused individuals who want to join our team as an Area Manager.

jobs.title: Area Manager II

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 5+ years of program or project management experience
- 5+ years of supply chain experience
- Experience using data and metrics to determine and drive improvements
- Experience owning program strategy, end to end delivery, and communicating results to senior leadership
- Bachelor's degree
jobs.description:
Join Amazon's dynamic environment as a Sr. Supply Chain Manager with a Network Capacity Background, where you will play a crucial role in shaping and optimizing various facets of our operations, concentrating on optimizing and managing the flow of inventory within the supply chain network. We have exciting opportunities across different business initiatives, each seeking a skilled professional to lead strategic initiatives and drive process improvements, with a specific focus on network capacity and the physical flow of goods or services within the supply chain network. As a Senior Supply Chain Manager, you will take end-to-end ownership of building and manifesting our capacity plan, operations, logistics optimization, network design, and analysis of operational efficiency.

In this role, you will collaborate with cross-functional teams, including Business, Operations, Supply Chain, Product and program teams in SCOT, Finance, Engineering and construction and more. You will utilize your analytical prowess to identify opportunities for operational improvements, implement scalable systems, and drive automation to enhance efficiency, with a particular focus on optimizing the physical flow and operations within the supply chain network. Whether you are managing network capacity initiatives, overseeing cross-functional programs, or focusing on strategic projects, your impact will be visible, ongoing, and contribute directly to our commitment to being the most customer-centric company on earth. The ideal candidate will not only have strong analytical skills and operational acumen, they will also be able to present and visualize data in a succinct manner and craft concise and comprehensive verbal and written narratives to influence executive decisions.

Key job responsibilities
- Lead strategic initiatives related to network capacity within Amazon's various business units
- Lead initiatives to optimize and manage the flow of inventory within the supply chain network
- Balance capacity and demand, assess and improve the efficiency of distribution networks, warehouses, and logistics operations
- Analyze and synthesize data to identify opportunities for enhancing customer experiences and operational efficiency in network capacity
- Define metrics, develop program management mechanisms, and invent/simplify processes to drive decision-making in complex network capacity situations
- Collaborate with stakeholders across different functions, influencing and facilitating agreements to achieve shared network capacity goals
- Manage cross-functional network capacity initiatives, ensuring continuous improvement and adherence to lean philosophy at scale and develop cadences to communicate progress to senior leaders
- Own a portfolio of cross-functional programs related to network capacity, driving performance improvement and efficiency in project delivery
- Identify and deliver operational improvement opportunities in network capacity, focusing on inventory management, logistics strategy, and transportation optimization
- Monitor and optimize key network capacity metrics to ensure the smooth flow of goods or services through the supply chain network

About the team
The Global Capacity Planning team's mission is to plan and deliver the North America Customer fulfillment capacity to meet the demand of our customers at desired speed and quality while improving the long term cost structure of the network. Our customers are: S&OP, PPT, Finance, FC operations, WW and Operations Engineering, Real Estate, WW Design, Launch, and Safety/EHS.

jobs.title: Area Manager - Theodore, AL

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 12+ years of finance or a related analytical field experience
- Bachelor's degree in accounting or related field
jobs.description:
The AWS Accounting organization is seeking a highly motivated Sr. Accounting Manager to lead a high-performing team and serve as a key business partner on the AWS Accounting Team. This role is central to strengthening controllership across the organization, with primary responsibility for managing and developing scalable financial controls and processes that simplify monthly reviews, enhance controllership rigor, and enable sustainable growth. You will partner closely with finance, accounting, tech and business teams to develop long-term strategies and drive new initiatives that support our mission, while working with key stakeholders and central teams to drive global standardization of metric and reporting definitions. This position requires collaboration across organizations to define financial and non-financial trade-offs in ambiguous environments, ensuring robust financial governance and control frameworks are maintained throughout. Additionally, you will lead and develop an exceptional Accounting team while actively investing in Finance and Accounting citizenship opportunities across the organization.

The right individual will have exceptional accounting skills, strong team leadership and people management capabilities, and solid business judgment capable of driving an organization to the right results. They will have operated successfully in a fast-paced, global, results-oriented environment, and have the ability to lead and develop a team while influencing the decisions of senior business leaders through effective verbal and written communication, logical reasoning, and the presentation of alternatives. The successful candidate will collaborate with partner accounting teams across the US and work closely with business and financial systems teams to support new launches and design and deliver high-impact AI initiatives and productivity improvements. They must be able to think and act both strategically and tactically while building and mentoring a high-performing accounting team.

Key job responsibilities
• Leading a US-based team in delivering accurate financial reporting as the primary accounting leader for the business area, directly influencing senior leaders
• Partnering and strategizing with business, accounting, finance, and tax stakeholders to ensure seamless coordination, compliance, and scalability
• Owning critical financial reporting deliverables, including 10-K and 10-Q disclosures, and presenting key findings to AWS senior accounting leadership
• Driving AI automation solutions and operational improvements to enhance efficiency and accuracy across accounting processes
• Collaborating with business teams to evaluate new AWS concepts and contracts, championing accounting controllership considerations in new business terms and launches
• Preparing and reviewing technical accounting memos under US GAAP, ensuring regulatory compliance
• Leading cross-functional projects including process improvements, controls enhancement, and integration of accounting processes with Amazon systems
• Coordinating quarterly reviews and approvals of estimates, presenting conclusions to senior leadership
• Executing quarterly reviews and sign-offs on SOX 404 key controls, ensuring robust internal control framework
jobs.description_short: The AWS Accounting organization is seeking a highly motivated Sr. Accounting Manager to lead a high-performing team and serve as a key business partner on the AWS Accounting Team. This role is central

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree, or 2+ years of professional experience
- • 2+ years digital design experience and execution
- • 2+ years background working in or alongside a collaborative multi-disciplinary team and working directly with other designers, writers, art directors, and/or marketing managers
- • Demonstrated proficiency with Adobe creative design software, such as Photoshop, Illustrator, After Effects, InDesign, etc.
jobs.description:
You? You’re a visual storyteller with an eye for creating a variety of eye-catching digital content, and you’re looking to join a fast-paced team that will feed you consistent opportunities to flex that creative muscle. Us? We’re Amazon’s Delivery Experience (ADX) team. We support and advocate for Amazon’s global network of delivery partners and Operations professionals, and we’re looking for a customer-obsessed Multimedia Designer to bring our messaging and brands to life across several bespoke channels.

Amazon’s Delivery Service Partners (DSPs) and Amazon Freight Partners (AFPs) enable Amazon to deliver millions of packages per week, for retail customers all across the world. The ADX team serves as a conduit between Amazon and these partners and internal Operations team members, communicating business critical information and program updates. This role will support communications efforts for both “community” types (DSP and AFP audiences) by driving production of bespoke, bar-raising multimedia content for a variety of platforms (digital community, live events, etc).

The Multimedia Designer role is best fit for a creative executor that can work both independently and collaboratively with diverse, cross-functional business teams. Our ideal candidate has the ability to dive deep into existing brand guidelines and produce great looking creative assets that meet or exceed our customer’s needs. Standouts in this role must possess a strong eye for design, proficiency in creating small-to-medium-sized creative assets in a variety of digital formats, and project management skills in order to prioritize and deliver against multiple, competing priorities.

Key job responsibilities
In this role, key responsibilities include:
• Collaborate with the Creative Director to adopt multiple brand guidelines and identities across a diverse set of creative media needs and formats
• Review internal Creative team intake form for new project assignments and track ongoing work
• Concept, design, revise, and deliver digital creative assets such as: logos, banners, infographics, gifs, video thumbnails, simple motion design, character design, PowerPoint presentation edits
• Adapt design layouts and deliverable types to best-fit the various specs and size requirements of the intended mediums
• Collaborate and communicate with all project requesters; facilitate discovery/kick off calls to better understand project needs and flag any potential red flags or watch-outs
• Submit design mocks and finals for brand review with brand management teams to ensure compliance and standards are met
• Identify and flag complex creative project asks to the ADX Creative Director for 3P agency assignment and execution
• Upon successful project completion, upload all assets to internal DAM platforms and tools

jobs.description:
Content Lab is the creative and content engine within Amazon's Delivery Training and Programs team. We meld UX and Adult Learning principles to strategize, develop, and maintain the Integrated Delivery Training Curriculum — preparing hundreds of thousands of Delivery Associates (DAs) each year across AMZL, AMXL, MMRO, and RSR operations. Through data-driven experimentation, we continuously push boundaries to elevate the training experience for our customer: the DA Trainee.
Our Design team owns brand and visual design across the Delivery Training Organization, producing consistent, high-quality experiences that show up for DAs every day across North America. We value craft, accuracy, and the ability to execute at scale without sacrificing standards.
We're looking for a Sr. Art Director who thrives in a hands-on production environment and brings both creative skill and operational discipline to their work. You'll partner closely with the Design Manager and the Design team to produce branded large-format graphics for Delivery Training classrooms and environmental graphics for stations across the NA market. You'll own refining the production process and refreshing our creative approach. This role is equal parts creative thinker and reliable executor — someone who can concept, present and defend their work to leadership with confidence, then build with precision.


Key job responsibilities
• Partner with the Design Manager, operations teams, and agency partners to develop and produce branded large-format graphics for training classrooms and environmental installations across NA stations
• Lead creative and execution for specialty placements, maintaining brand consistency and accuracy across all outputs
• Art direct photo and video shoots, ensuring deliverables meet brand standards and project requirements
• Art direct motion graphics and animation projects in collaboration with internal teams and external partners
• Mentor designers and provide clear, constructive creative feedback that raises the quality of team output


A day in the life
No two days look exactly the same for the Content Lab Art Director — and that's by design. This role sits at the intersection of brand, production, and creative leadership, moving fluidly between strategic thinking and hands-on execution depending on what the team and the business need most.

A typical day might start with a morning standup with the Design Manager and Design team, reviewing active projects across the NA network rollout — checking status on large-format classroom graphics for an iLMDA site launch, or aligning on timelines for an upcoming graphic installations with operations teams. Midday could bring a creative review with an agency partner, providing clear direction on a round of revisions. You may get into the files and building mechanicals or creating illustrations. You may upload new assets to our Digital Asset Management tool and execute admin functions.

Woven throughout the day are moments of mentorship — a quick feedback session with a junior designer on a layout, a conversation about brand application, or a review of a designer's work-in-progress with constructive, specific direction. When content design needs surge, the Art Director steps in to support production directly, keeping the team's output moving without missing a beat.

At the end of the day, this role is about showing up as both a creative leader and a reliable executor — someone who can hold the brand standard, support the team, and deliver at the scale Amazon requires.

About the team
The Content Lab is the in-house creative team within Amazon's Delivery Training and Programs (DT&P) organization, responsible for designing and producing the branded learning environments, visual communications, and training materials that support DSP Delivery Associates across the North American network.

Our team operates at the intersection of brand, design, and operations — translating complex training programs into clear, engaging, and on-brand physical and digital experiences. From large-format classroom graphics and environmental installations to station signage, photo and video productions, and VR training content, we build the visual infrastructure that helps hundreds of thousands of Delivery Associates learn, grow, and succeed in their roles.

We are a small, high-output team that values craft, collaboration, and creative problem-solving. We work closely with operations partners, program managers, and agency collaborators to deliver work that is both strategically sound and visually excellent. We take pride in the quality of what we make and the impact it has on the people who experience it every day.

If you're someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment, cares deeply about brand and design quality, and wants your work to reach people at scale — this is the team for you.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree or above in design, human-computer interaction (HCI), or Associate's degree or above
- 10+ years of experience as a user experience designer, product designer, interaction designer, information architect, or similar role
- Portfolio that demonstrates delivering end-to-end experiences for digital services across multiple platforms and devices
- Experience in a range of design approaches, methods, and tools that you use to plan, concept, and prototype complex projects
jobs.description:
At Audible, we believe stories have the power to transform lives. It’s why we work with some of the world’s leading creators to produce and share audio storytelling with our millions of global listeners. We are dreamers and inventors who come from a wide range of backgrounds and experiences to empower and inspire each other. Imagine your future with us.

ABOUT THIS ROLE
Revolutionizing the storytelling experience is just part of an Audible UX Designer’s daily to-do list. You’ll craft refined interface elements and improve usability for harmonious listening anytime, anywhere, on any platform – including iOS, Android, web, and Alexa. Like us, you’re customer obsessed, and every decision you make will be shaped by your profound understanding and curiosity of the customer experience. Just when it seems a product is perfected, you’ll envision a thoughtful new way to take it further. Your creativity will take deep customer insight and transform it into innovative experiences that will continuously evolve the UX, all while discovering new ways to unleash the power of the spoken word.

ABOUT YOU
Life inspires your work. You look to yourself for insight, and can’t help keeping a running list of ways to improve your own UX. You’re most creative when working through situational restraints, from everyday life hacks to professional milestones. You always strive for more, an expert in proactive design thinking. No stone is ever left unturned; you’re all about asking the big questions and the small ones. The only thing more exciting than validating a solution is sharing it with your team. And as a collaborator and mentor, you’ve seen first hand how constructive feedback and a growth mindset create an environment where everyone thrives. You’re ready to hone your craft and broaden your design skills, but the journey with your team is what drives you.

As a Principal UX Designer, you will....
- Envision and bring to life intuitive, scalable, and engaging experiences
- Facilitate work sessions with executive leaders
- Champion human-centered design culture
- Drive creative process and collaboration with cross-functional partners to propose new ideas and strategies
- Provide coaching and feedback, with an emphasis on growing junior designers into future leaders
- Create and drive adoption of design guidelines, mechanisms, best practices, process, and tools

ABOUT AUDIBLE
Audible is the leading producer and provider of audio storytelling. We spark listeners’ imaginations, offering immersive, cinematic experiences full of inspiration and insight to enrich our customers daily lives. We are a global company with an entrepreneurial spirit. We are dreamers and inventors who are passionate about the positive impact Audible can make for our customers and our neighbors. This spirit courses throughout Audible, supporting a culture of creativity and inclusion built on our People Principles and our mission to build more equitable communities in the cities we call home.
jobs.description_short: At Audible, we believe stories have the power to transform lives. It’s why we work with some of the world’s leading creators to produce and share audio storytelling with our millions of global listeners.
